title: Trajectory Optimization
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Trajectory Optimization: Trajectory optimization: find minimum-time or minimum-effort robot trajectory satisfying dynamics and obstacle constraints. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.collocation.direct_transcription…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~2000; dynamic_obstacle_uncertainty, model_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: min integral_0 ** T l(x,u) dt; s.t. dx/dt = f(x,u), g(x,u) <= 0 (constraints), x(0)=x0, x(T)=xT
E.world_state_x: optimal state-control trajectory {x*(t), u*(t)} over time horizon T
E.observation_y: initial/final conditions, obstacle set, dynamics model

W.regime: Existence of the recovered optimal_trajectory is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 2000); dynamic_obstacle_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Deterministic sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: cost within 5% of global optimum for convex costs; local minima for nonconvex. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~2000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |dynamic_obstacle_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_nodes * n_state ** 2 * N_iter) for direct methods per iteration
